title: CSS Display
content:
### CSS Display

**display** controls how an element behaves in the layout.

---

### Basic Display Values

| Value | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `block` | Takes full width, starts new line |
| `inline` | Takes only content width, in line |
| `inline-block` | Inline but can set width/height |
| `none` | Removed from layout (invisible) |
| `flex` | Block-level flex container |
| `inline-flex` | Inline-level flex container |
| `grid` | Block-level grid container |
| `inline-grid` | Inline-level grid container |
| `table` | Behaves like `<table>` |
| `list-item` | Behaves like `<li>` |
| `contents` | Removes box (children take its place) |

---

### Block Elements

```css
/* Default: div, p, h1-h6, section, article */
display: block;
```

- Stretch full width of parent
- Start on new line
- Respect width, height, margin, padding

---

### Inline Elements

```css
/* Default: span, a, strong, em, img */
display: inline;
```

- Only as wide as content
- No new line
- **Ignore width/height**
- Only horizontal margin/padding

---

### Inline-Block

```css
display: inline-block;
```

Best of both:
- Stays inline (no line break)
- Respects width/height/margin/padding

---

### Display: None vs Visibility: Hidden

```css
.hidden {
  display: none;       /* Removed from flow */
  visibility: hidden;  /* Invisible but takes space */
}
```

---

### Common Patterns

```css
/* Hide element */
.hide { display: none; }

/* Horizontal navigation */
nav li {
  display: inline-block;
}

/* Center inline-block */
.center-inline {
  text-align: center;
}
.center-inline > * {
  display: inline-block;
}
```

> **Key Point:** `display` values like `flex` and `grid` change how **children** behave. `display: none` removes element from layout entirely.
codeExample:
Block - takes full width
Another block
Inline 1 Inline 2 (width ignored)
Inline-block 1
Inline-block 2

quiz.question: Which display value hides an element WITHOUT removing its space?
